page: Microsoft Azure E80is_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the XEISv4 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 6.3 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI.

This server is designated as isolated and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any local storage. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or onboard GPU hardware. The high memory capacity relative to vCPUs positions this instance as a strong candidate for memory-intensive enterprise applications.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng score of 74314.11 and a passmark CPU score of 53311.42. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 4154.6 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 96.19 GB/sec. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 64.02 million operations per second. However, LLM text generation performance is poor, with the medium 7B model generating only 5.56 tokens per second, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E80is_v4 offers a dense allocation of memory and dedicated vCPUs, making it a viable option for large relational databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines. The isolation feature provides security and performance guarantees for sensitive workloads. It is best suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression tasks, while being poorly suited for real-time LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure E80is_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the XEISv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs across 40 physical cores, 504.0 GB of RAM, and a 35 MB L3 cache, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks demonstrate strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, with Redis SET operations reaching 64.02 million operations per second and a passmark CPU score of 53311.42. Conversely, LLM text generation performance is poor due to the absence of hardware acceleration. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale database operations, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E80is_v4 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E80is_v4 is an isolated, memory-optimized VM with 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504 GB RAM, designed for demanding database workloads.
tagline: An isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring eighty dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM
Isolated instance type running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Optimized for large databases, ERP systems, and heavy data processing
Poorly suited for GPU-dependent tasks and real-time LLM text generation
